RAM (Random Access Memory) is used to store temporary data while processing instructions, while ROM (Read Only Memory) is used to store permanent data that cannot be changed or erased. RAM is volatile, meaning it loses its contents when the power goes off, whereas ROM is non-volatile and remains intact even if the power goes out.
